title: Flock Event Logs

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Event Logs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request that your agency produce for inspection and copying the following public record(s) within three (3) business days of receipt of this request, as required by law: Records Requested: The complete Flock Safety Event Log from the earliest available date through the date this record is received. I request that these records be provided in electronic format (e.g., PDF, CSV, or native file format) via email, if possible. If electronic production is not feasible, please notify me and I will make arrangements for physical inspection or copying. If there are any fees associated with the search, retrieval, or copying of these records, please provide an itemized fee estimate before processing this request. If the total cost is less than $25.00, please proceed without prior notification. If any portion of this request is denied, in whole or in part, please cite the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each denial and notify me of the right to appeal such denial, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72. Please acknowledge receipt of this request in writing as soon as practicable. Under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(1)(A), your agency must produce the requested records or deny this request within three (3) business days of receipt. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Index of TERA, MCAN, and Tier I/II biotech submissions for environmental release, FY2021–present

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Index of TERA, MCAN, and Tier I/II biotech submissions for environmental release, FY2021–present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list, index, log, tracking spreadsheet, or equivalent record identifying all of the following submissions received by EPA where the submission concerns environmental release, field testing, or any intended use of the microorganism outside of contained structures:

TSCA Experimental Release Applications (TERAs)
Microbial Commercial Activity Notices (MCANs)
Tier I exemption notices
Tier II exemption notices

For each submission, I request the following non-CBI fields to the extent maintained:

Case number or docket identifier
Submission title or short description
Date of receipt
Submitter name (where not designated as CBI)
Type of submission (TERA, MCAN, Tier I, or Tier II)
Status or disposition (e.g., under review, withdrawn, dropped from review, completed, expired)
Date of final EPA action, if any
Intended use category or organism type, where captured in tracking records

I am specifically excluding submissions limited to contained use (e.g., fermentor-based industrial production with no environmental release component).
Date range: October 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed.
If no single tracking record exists, please provide the closest equivalent (e.g., entries exported from the Biotechnology Program's case management system).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty

communications.subject: Receipt of EPA National FOIA Office FOIA Request
communications.communication:
Dear Niko McCarty , 

Thank you for submitting your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request. 
Case Number 2026-EPA-05039 has been assigned to this request. In all future correspondence regarding this request, please reference your case number. 
If you selected EPA National FOIA Office (for all non-OIG requests), your request was transmitted directly to the EPA’s National FOIA Office (NFO). NFO will confirm receipt and assignment of your request by sending you an Acknowledgement email within five business days. 
If you have any questions, please contact the FOIA Requester Service Center at hq.foia@epa.gov (mailto:hq.foia@epa.gov) or at 202-566-1667.
Regards, 
EPA National FOIA Office

title: Public Records Request — CMGC2024-105 Complete Procurement File

communications.subject: Idaho Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request — CMGC2024-105 Complete Procurement File
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Idaho Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The complete procurement file for solicitation CMGC2024-105,
Recycled Water Program Improvements Project (RWP 010), including:

1. NAMES AND AFFILIATIONS: The names, titles, departmental affiliations,
   and qualifications of all evaluation committee members who reviewed
   and scored responses to this solicitation.

2. INDIVIDUAL SCORE SHEETS: The individual evaluator score sheets
   showing the scores assigned by each committee member to each
   proposing firm, including any written comments, notes, or
   justifications recorded by evaluators during scoring.

3. FINAL SCORES AND RANKING: The final consensus score sheet and
   complete ranking of all firms that submitted proposals.

4. INTENT TO AWARD: The Intent to Award letter and all documentation
   supporting the award recommendation, including the stated contract
   value, scope of services, and any conditions or contingencies.

5. COUNCIL APPROVAL DETERMINATION: Any written determination, analysis,
   memorandum, or correspondence addressing whether this award required
   Boise City Council approval, including any dollar threshold, statutory
   provision, or delegation authority cited as the basis for the award
   authority used.

6. BID PROTEST RECORDS: Any bid protests, objections, or challenges
   received from proposing firms, and the city's responses or
   determinations regarding each.

7. EVALUATION COMMITTEE CORRESPONDENCE: All correspondence (including
   emails, memoranda, meeting notes, and written instructions) between
   the Purchasing division and the evaluation committee members regarding
   this solicitation, including any direction provided to evaluators
   about scoring methodology, evaluation criteria weighting, or
   treatment of specific proposers.

8. SIGNED CONTRACT: The final signed contract or agreement between the
   City of Boise and the awarded firm, including:
   a. The complete contract document with all exhibits, appendices,
      and attachments
   b. The pre-construction services agreement or scope
   c. The payment schedule, fee structure, and any guaranteed maximum
      price provisions
   d. All amendments, change orders, or modifications executed to date
   e. The stated total contract value or not-to-exceed amount

9. CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURES: Any conflict-of-interest disclosure
   forms, ethics certifications, or recusal statements signed by or
   pertaining to each evaluation committee member in connection with
   this solicitation.

10. SOLICITATION REVIEW FORM: The solicitation review form, procurement
    checklist, or equivalent internal document used to document and
    verify that each step of the procurement process was completed in
    accordance with city policy.

11. OFFEROR LIST: The list of all firms that attended any pre-proposal
    conference, submitted questions during the solicitation period,
    or requested solicitation documents.

12. SHORT LIST DOCUMENTATION: Any documentation of a short list
    determination, including the criteria used to select firms for
    interviews and the firms selected.

Date range for correspondence (Items 5, 7): January 1, 2024 through
December 31, 2024.

Please provide records in electronic format (PDF of original documents
preferred; native format such as Excel for score sheets if available).

If any record or portion of a record is withheld, please identify each
withheld record by date, author, recipient, and subject matter, and
cite the specific statutory exemption justifying each withholding, as
required by Idaho Code § 74-113.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Smith

communications.subject: Acknowledgement of Public Records Request # 2026-1643
communications.communication:
​​ 
  
 DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY CLERK​​​ 
 
 

 PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGMENT 

  PRRID: 2026-1643 

 Thank you! The City of Boise has received your request for public records submitted on 05/27/2026.  The full details of your request is contained at the bottom of this email. 
 

 Please know that the City of Boise is committed to transparent and ethical government and is currently working to respond to your request within three (3) business days. Pursuant to Idaho Code § 74-103, the City may take up to ten (10) business days to locate the requested records. If the request cannot be provided within the allocated time period, you will receive written notice from the City. 
 

 If additional information or clarification is needed, the City will contact you for the necessary details to fulfill your request. 
 As outlined in Idaho Code § 74-102(10)(b), if processing your request for records requires more than two hours of labor or the number of pages being requested exceeds one hundred pages (100), you will receive a cost estimate outlining the fees that must be paid before your request can be processed. Your request will not be processed until the City receives payment.  If your request takes less time to fulfill than estimated, you will be refunded the difference between the estimated and actual cost. Should your request take longer to process than expected, you will receive an additional cost letter.  If the City does not receive the payment within 30 days of the cost letter being sent, the request will be deemed withdrawn. 

 
 
 If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our office. 
 Thank you, 

Kylie Boazman, Department of the City Clerk 
(208) 972-8149 or email prr_cityclerk@cityofboise.org​​ 
​​​

NAMES AND AFFILIATIONS: The names, titles, departmental affiliations, and qualifications of alL evaluation committee members who reviewed and scored responses to this solicitation. 2. INDIVIDUAL SCORE SHEETS: The individual evaluator score sheets showing the scores assigned by each committee member to each proposing firm, including any written comments, notes, or justifications recorded by evaluators during scoring. 3. FINAL SCORES AND RANKING: The final consensus score sheet and complete ranking of all firms that submitted proposals. 4. INTEND TO AWARD: The Intent to Award letter and all documentation supporting the award recommendation, including the stated contract value, scope of services, and any conditions or contingencies. 5. COUNCIL APPROVAL DETERMINATION: Any written determination, analysis, memorandum, or correspondence addressing whether this award required Boise City Council approval, including any dollar threshold, statutory provision, or delegation authority cited as the basis for the award authority used. 6. BID PROTEST RECORDS: Any bid protests, objections, or challenges received from proposing firms, and the city's responses or determination regarding each. 7. EVALUATION COMMITTEE CORRESPONDENCE: All correspondence (including emails, memoranda, meeting notes, and written instructions) between the Purchasing division and the evaluation committee members regarding this solicitation, including any direction provided to evaluators about scoring methodology, evaluation criteria weighting, or treatment of specific proposers. 8. SIGNED CONTRACT: the final signed contract or agreement between the City of Boise and the awarded firm, including:     a. The complete contract document with all exhibits, appendices, and attachments     b. The pre-construction services agreement or scope     c. The payment schedule, fee structure, and any guaranteed maximum price provisions     d. All amendments, change orders, or modifications executed to date     e. The stated total contract value or not-to-exceed amount9. CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURES: Any conflict-of-interest disclosure forms, ethics certifications, or recusal statements signed by or pertaining to each evaluation committee member in connection with this solicitation. 10. SOLICITATION REVIEW FORM: The solicitation review form, procurement checklist, or equivalent internal document used to document and verify that each step of the procurement process was completed in accordance with city policy. 11. OFFEROR LIST: The list of all firms that attended any pre-proposal conference, submitted questions during the solicitation period, or requested solicitation documents. 12. SHORT LIST DOCUMENTATION: Any documentation of a short list determination, including the criteria used to select firms for interviews and the firms selected. Please provide records in electronic format (PDF of original documents preferred; native format such as Excel for score sheets if available).If any record or portion of a record is withheld, please identify each withheld record by date, author, recipient, and subject matter, and cite the specific statutory exemption justifying each withholding, as required by Idaho Code 74-113. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. ​  
  
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY 
 
 

 RESPONSE FILES READY​ 

  PRRID: 2026-1643 Smith- CMGC2024-105 
ACCESS EMAIL: 211692-79466475@requests.muckrock.com 

The response to your public records request has been uploaded to a secure distribution website.  Files are availablefor review or download for 14 days via the button below. 

​ Open 2026-1643 Response​​ ​​ (https://boisecity.sharepoint.com/:f:/s/cw.rdc/IgAWoTnV45b8QqQNe23l2IiyASrY1ElRebltF-timSHFbXg) 
​​​​ ​​ 
Thank you for your Public Records Request.  City of Boise personnel located the attached documents responsive to your request.  With respect to the 12 numbered items you provided, please note the following: 
  * We do not release the name of evaluators. As we have had in the past, requestors reach out to the evaluator's wanting more information and harassing them because of their reviews.  Project Manager names are in the Specifications. 

  * Individual Score sheets have been uploaded 

  * All score sheets have been uploaded 

  * ITA uploaded 

  * Contract – first 4 pages are Council RES Approval 

  * No protests were received. 

  * Responsive emails have been included. - Instructions given on the interview are uploaded. Evaluation was done online via Jaggaer 

  * Executed Contract and Change order #1 have been uploaded 

  * There are no conflict of Interest 

  * Solicitation (specifications ) have been uploaded as well as the plan holders list.  All steps for the sourcing event are tracked and monitored via Jaggaer Software. These can be tracked on the specifications 

  * Prebid attendance list has been uploaded 

  * Short list is second ITA document 

Please feel free to contact me with any questions.  Thank you, Todd Van Horn   tvanhorn@cityofboise.org 

 

 ​Any release of public records (textual, audio, video, graphical, pictorial, digital, or otherwise) in response to your public records request is not permission from the City to allow you to republish or otherwise make use of the records in violation of state or federal law, including privacy, trademark, or copyright law. 
 

 If you have any questions regarding this response, please do not hesitate to contact me. 
​ 
Thank you, 

Office of the City Attorney

title: Contract Request: VENDOR PRODUCT California Polytechnic University

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Contract Request: VENDOR PRODUCT California Polytechnic University
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with Elsevier relating to the licensing of Interfolio;
All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; 
All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with Elsevier for faculty information system technology;
and
Assessment documentation including, but not limited to, the Higher Education Community Vendor Assessment Toolkit (HECVAT). 

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of Interfolio.

The scope of this request should encompass more than just direct contracts or renewals between the university and Elsevier; please incorporate all applicable agreements and supporting records facilitated through consortium entities (such as Lyrasis).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrea Puglisi

title: March 5, 2025 Threat, Hancock High School

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: March 5, 2025 Threat, Hancock High School
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

On March 5, 2025 there were threats made to Hancock High School. The threat was publicly reported (https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2025/03/05/police-cpd-schools-swatting). 

1. All incident reports related to the threats made to Hancock High School on March 5, 2025. 
2. Any emails that were sent to the school community about the threat made on March 5, 2025. 
3. Any emails between Trista Harper and Safety and Security or CPS Legal related to the threat made on March 5, 2025. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2281443/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
June 10, 2026 Miriam Bhimani 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019537-050526 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request seeking the following information:

"On March 5, 2025 there were threats made to Hancock High School. The threat was publicly reported (https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2025/03/05/police-cpd-schools-swatting).

1. All incident reports related to the threats made to Hancock High School on March 5, 2025.
2. Any emails that were sent to the school community about the threat made on March 5, 2025.
3. Any emails between Trista Harper and Safety and Security or CPS Legal related to the threat made on March 5, 2025.

RESPONSE: Records responsive to your FOIA request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center. Please visit the link and enter your username and password to access the records. http://cps.edu/About_CPS/Departments/Law/Pages/FOIARequest.aspx. Be advised that additional responsive emails can be found in your FOIA Request N019538-050526.

Records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(f) exempts any “Preliminary drafts, notes, recommendations, memoranda and other records in which opinions are expressed, or policies or actions are formulated, except that a specific record or relevant portion of a record shall not be exempt when the record is publicly cited and identified by the head of the public body.”

 

Please be advised that student information is exempt under FOIA and records have been redacted pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, section 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). 

 

To the extent you consider this a denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706.  You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court; 5 ILCS 140/11.

  

Thank you for your request.

 

Sincerely,

 

 

Ana Diaz

Freedom of Information Act Officer

Chicago Public Schools"

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Island County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Island County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Island County public records request #26-211
communications.communication:
Island County ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-211: Good morning. This e-mail serves as our acknowledgement to your Public Records Request dated January 29, 2026, pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, in which you asked for requested/stated: “Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.”.” I have done a cursory glance through our Use of Force information as we do not have a report that encompasses the parameters of what you are requesting but instead, I have located approximately 349 Use of Force reports over the timeframe you are requesting, with an estimate page count of 1,542. Of note, these Use of Force reports are only for the patrol side of the Island County Sheriff’s Office and not the Island County Jail.  We process records requests in the order in which they are received, and your request is currently in the queue. I estimate six to eight months  will be required to review the records for responsiveness and possible exemptions and redactions, and to assemble the final collection of documents. I estimate a first installment on or before May 31, 2026. It may help to understand that Island County is comprised of multiple “agencies” for the purposes of the Public Records Act. This is because county government is an amalgam of independently elected officials who maintain exclusive control over their areas of authority, with respect to other county officials. We maintain exclusive control over the records that pertain to the operation of the Island County Sheriff’s Office.  Per RCW 42.56.120, public records requests are subject to payment of reasonable charges for any records provided. The current rates, as established by Island County Ordinance C-46-22 https://islandcountywa.gov/516/Public-Records-Requests (scroll to bottom of page). An estimated cost, currently, is $20.00. For questions or additional information pertaining to this request, please reply to this email or contact our office at (360) 678-4422. Regards,  Mary Kaye Weller Civil/Public Records Clerk Island County Sheriff's Office ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Island County.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.communication:
Dear e e: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the City of San Marcos. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act. Your request was given the reference number W029374-060426  for tracking purposes.  
 Records Requested: 
Other Information 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

A copy of all documents, including but not limited to, applications, agreements, and reporting documents related to the award for the FY25 SB224 Catalytic Converter administered by the Motor Vehicle Crime Prevention Authority. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2308894/ 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant City department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. 
 PLEASE NOTE: The Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. The City of San Marcos will abide by the Attorney General's Cost Rules Section 552.261 and charge accordingly. 
 If you need the following: 
 
  * Requesting information about a school, public or private  , you need to file your request with the school or school district. 
  * Requesting a Zoning Verification Letter  , please go to www.mygovernmentonline.org to make a request. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
 
City Clerk’s Staff 
City of San Marcos, Texas To monitor the progress or update this request please log in to the "My Request Center" on the . San Marcos Records Center

title: Request to Air Force for 2019 budget report for Public Affairs Agency

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Air Force for 2019 budget report for Public Affairs Agency
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The 2019 annual budget report for the Public Affairs Agency of the U.S. Air Force.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Request Acknowledgement - AF 2026-02599-F
communications.communication:
Dear Brett  Carollo, 
This is to acknowledge receipt of your January 28, 2026, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request for "The 2019 annual budget report for the Public Affairs Agency of the U.S. Air Force.I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies.""(Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2015 To 01/28/2026). Based on our initial review, we believe we cannot process your request within the statutory 20 workdays because our organization has a significant number of pending FOIA requests.  To ensure all requesters are properly served in the order their requests were received, we have instituted multitrack processing of requests.  Based on the information you provided, we have placed your request in the complex track. The Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) provides for the collection of fees based on the costs of processing a FOIA request and your fee category.  Based upon the information provided in your request, we have placed you in the Other category. We have assigned number 2026-02599-F to identify your request; should you need to contact us about your request.  Please direct your questions to the HAF FOIA team at SAF.AA.HAF.FOIA.Workflow@us.af.mil and use this assigned number to assist us in responding more promptly. We will strive to provide an update between 90 and 120 days.  Our policy is to process requests within their respective tracks in the order in which they were received in accordance with 32 CFR, Part 286.8.  We will notify you in writing if additional processing time is required.  We do process each FOIA request as quickly as possible, however delayed responses can be expected. Very Respectfully, SAF/AMGI FOIA TEAM Office of the Secretary of the Air Force 1000 Air Force Pentagon Washington, DC 20330-1000

communications.subject: AF FOIA Update - AF 2026-02599-F
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Carollo, 
Our office is following up with you concerning your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated January 28, 2026, regarding the 2019 annual budget report for the Public Affairs Agency of the U.S. Air Force. Our office is tracking this action under Air Force case number 2026-02599-F. 
We are still actively processing your request and are currently awaiting a response from the relevant action office. Consequently, additional time is required to properly complete the processing of your inquiry. Please be assured that we are working to fulfill your request as expeditiously as possible. Due to an elevated volume of requests, we appreciate your continued patience.

communications.subject: Final Response - AF FOIA 2026-02599-F
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Carollo, 
This letter is the final response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated January 28, 2026, in which you requested the 2019 annual budget report for the Public Affairs Agency of the U.S. Air Force. 
If you are dissatisfied with the servicing of this request or in this response from this office, you may contact the Air Force FOIA Public Liaison Officer, Mr. Robert Bivins, concerning this response at AF FOIA Public Liaison Office (SAF/CNZA), 1800 Air Force Pentagon, Washington, DC 20330-1800, DAF.FOIA@US.AF.MIL or (703) 614-8500. Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at OGIS@NARA.GOV; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. 

Please contact this office at the address above, via email at SAF.AA.HAF.FOIA.Workflow@us.af.mil, should you have any questions and refer to case AF 2026-02599-F.  Department of Defense Regulation 5400.7/Air Force Manual 33-302 indicates fees may be assessed for processing this request; however, there are no applicable fees in this instance. 

Sincerely, 
SAF/AAGI FOIA TEAM Government Information Management Branch (SAF/AAGI) Office of the Secretary of the Air Force 1000 Air Force Pentagon Washington, DC 20330-1000

communications.subject: RE: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Compensation Agreements since 2019 + Project Thor
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Ohio Open Records Law request, copied below, and originally submitted on May 14, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: 2-page fax received from +1 458-200-3365
communications.communication:
I am writing in response to your public-records request pursuant to Ohio
Revised Code 149.43. After a reasonable search, responsive records, to the
extent they could be identified, are enclosed.

The request is denied in part because several categories, including
requests for “all” records of a particular type and similar catch-all
categories over a multi-month period, are overly broad and ambiguous and do
not reasonably identify specific records as required by *R.C.*
149.43(B)(2). *See* *State ex rel. Morgan v. New Lexington*,
2006-Ohio-6365. The Ohio Public Records Act "does not contemplate that an
individual has the right to a complete duplication of voluminous files kept
by government agencies." *See State ex rel. Glasgow v. Jones*,
2008-Ohio-4788. Instead, Ohio law provides that a requester must identify
the records that he or she is seeking "with reasonable clarity." *Id.* A
request must not be overly broad and must describe what is requested
"specifically and particularly.” *Id.* In providing a response to a
request, a public office is not required "to do research or to identify
records containing selected information." *See Gupta v. City of Cleveland*,
Ct. of Cl. No. 2017-00840PQ, 2018-Ohio-3475 (holding requests for "entire
categories of records" for multiple years overly broad).

The request is also denied in part to the extent it seeks records protected
from disclosure by the attorney-client privilege. Communications seeking or
conveying legal advice, as well as attorney work product or other materials
prepared by counsel in connection with legal representation, are not
subject to release under the Public Records Act. *See State ex rel. Leslie
v. Ohio Hous. Fin. Agency*, 2005-Ohio-1508.

The request is further denied in part to the extent it seeks records that
do not exist or would require the District to create a new record,
compilation, or report in order to respond. *R.C.* 149.43 requires
production of existing, identifiable records kept by the public office; it
does not require a public office to create records that are not maintained.
See *State ex rel. Lanham v. Smith*, 2007-Ohio-609.

The District has made a good faith effort to respond to your request with
records deemed responsive within a reasonable time. If you would like to
revise or narrow any portion of the request, please do so in writing, and
the District will review any clarified request in accordance with the
Public Records Act.



Sincerely,



Trey Fausnaugh

Teays Valley Local School District

385 Viking Way

Ashville, Ohio 43103

*tfausnaugh@tvsd.us <tfausnaugh@tvsd.us>*

Phone: 740.983.5012











XMediusFAX Cloud

2-page fax received from +1 458-200-3365























*Received Time:*

Jun 1, 2026, 1:06:24 AM

*To (DID):*

+1 740-983-4158

-- 
This e-mail message, including any attachments, is for the sole use of
the 
intended recipient(s) and may contain private, confidential, and/or
privileged information. Any unauthorized review, use, disclosure, or
distribution is prohibited. If you are not the intended recipient, 
employee, or
agent responsible for delivering this message, please contact 
the sender by
reply e-mail and destroy all copies of the original e-mail 
message.

title: Department of Defense support for TV series "Murder at the Motel"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for TV series "Murder at the Motel"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the TV series "Murder at the Motel" (2024-). Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Murder at the Motel," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Murder at the Motel" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #25-F-3173
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern,

This message pertains to FOIA request 25-F-3173. I am appealing the redaction of third-party names and other information from the document released to me, which is a PAA signed between the DoD and Peninsula Television for the TV documentary series "Murder at the Motel." 

In the Final Response to my request, "personal privacy" was cited as the reason for all the redactions. In the case of third-party signatories to an agreement signed with the federal government, there should not be any expectation of privacy, I argue. And, in any case, the public interest here should outweigh any expectations of privacy on the part of individuals acting as agents of a production company signing an agreement with the federal government that is subject to FOIA.

I therefore request that this same PAA be released to me without any redactions of third-party information.

Very Respectfully,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: FOIA Appeal Acknowledgement: 25-A-3173-A1 (FOIA Request 25-F-3173)
communications.communication:
Good morning,



This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) appeal in reference to your OSD/JS FOIA request 25-F-3173.  We received your FOIA appeal on July 22, 2025, and have assigned your appeal tracking number 25-A-3173-A1.  Please refer to this number when communicating about your request.



Due to an extremely heavy FOIA workload, we are unable to complete your appeal within the statutory time requirement.  In fairness to the general public, we make every effort to treat all requesters equally.  Accordingly, responses are made on a first-in, first-out basis.  When the appellate review of your case is complete, you will be notified by our office of the final determination.  You may direct any questions concerning this appeal to:  osd.mc-alex.oatsd-pclt.mbx.dfoipo-foia-appeals@mail.mil<mailto:osd.mc-alex.oatsd-pclt.mbx.dfoipo-foia-appeals@mail.mil>. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records maintained by Alma Fuerte Public School for the period January 1, 2023 through the present relating to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on school property, including work performed with or without a formal contract, and regardless of dollar amount.

Specifically, I am requesting:

- Contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, work authorizations, or informal arrangements for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities-related work performed on campus, including contracts under $5,000 that are maintained internally by the school.

- Procurement and payment records, including invoices, purchase orders, reimbursements, receipts, stipends, or any other records reflecting compensation or consideration for facilities or construction work.

- Contractor or worker documentation, including business names, individual names, contractor license numbers, proof of license, insurance certificates, bonding documentation, or records reflecting whether work was performed by licensed or unlicensed individuals.

- Internal approvals or authorizations, including administrative approvals or governing board approvals, for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work.

- Permits, inspection reports, or compliance documentation associated with any construction, renovation, or facilities projects.

- Email correspondence or written communications between school administrators, staff, or governing board members regarding construction, renovation, repair, facilities work, or procurement related to campus improvements.

This request seeks copies of existing records only and does not require the creation of new records.
If any portion of this request is denied or redacted, please identify the specific exemption relied upon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Steve Bennett

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for your response and for providing the purchase order records.

The list provided appears to consist primarily of general procurement and supply purchases from Amazon and do not address the specific categories requested related to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on campus.

To clarify, the request sent on 01/15/2026 is seeking records of:

- Contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, work authorizations, or informal arrangements for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities-related work performed on campus, including contracts under $5,000 that are maintained internally by the school.

- Procurement and payment records, including invoices, purchase orders, reimbursements, receipts, stipends, or any other records reflecting compensation or consideration for facilities or construction work.

- Contractor or worker documentation, including business names, individual names, contractor license numbers, proof of license, insurance certificates, bonding documentation, or records reflecting whether work was performed by licensed or unlicensed individuals.

- Internal approvals or authorizations, including administrative approvals or governing board approvals, for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work.

- Permits, inspection reports, or compliance documentation associated with any construction, renovation, or facilities projects.

- Email correspondence or written communications between school administrators, staff, or governing board members regarding construction, renovation, repair, facilities work, or procurement related to campus improvements.

If no such records exist for the requested time period of January 1, 2023 through present, please confirm that no responsive records exist.

Otherwise, please provide any responsive records or indicate when they will be made available.

As a follow-up to the original request submitted on January 10, 2026, please provide an update on the status of the request consistent with the timelines set forth under the California Public Records Act.

Thank you for your assistance.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello,

I am writing regarding my Public Records Act request submitted on 01/15/2026, and follow-up on 04/14/2026.

To date, no response has been received addressing the specific categories requested related to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on your campus.

Under the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code §6253(c)), agencies are required to make a determination within 10 calendar days regarding whether responsive records exist and to promptly notify the requester.

The materials previously provided did not address the requested categories, and no further clarification or response has been received following my follow-up.

Accordingly, please:

Confirm whether responsive records related to the request exist, and
Provide any responsive records, or a clear statement that no responsive records exist.

If additional time is required to produce records, please provide a specific date by which responsive records will be made available.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

communications.subject: Re: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Bennett:

We are in receipt of your request. Please be advised that your earlier
emails were intercepted by the organization's spam filters and did not make
its way to its intended destination. We are now in receipt of your request
and are processing the request accordingly.

We confirm that responsive records exist, however, the nature of the
request appears to be voluminous and broad and is expected to take an
extended time to collect and produce all potentially responsive records as
written. However, we understand from the context and subsequent information
we have received that the request is seeking the below-listed information
specifically in the context of work performed by Done and Done (the
"Subject").

Can you confirm if the request can be considered narrowed as to documents
concerning the Subject?  We make this request pursuant to Government Code
Section 79200.600 "in order to assist the member of the public make a
focused and effective request."  We anticipate that with the scope focused
on the Subject, it would be possible to produce responsive records within
approximately two-three weeks. Any broader scope would be expected to take
a period of months given that it would require a manual search of a
significant volume of records in physical and electronic format by limited
staffing resources (the responding agency is a public school).

This response is without waiver of any privileges or exemptions that may be
claimed as responsive documents are reviewed.

Thank you,
Adriani Leon

-- 

Adriani Leon,
Community Schools Director, Hablo Español!
almafuerteps.org

Alma Fuerte is a public school with a focus on entrepreneurship!
We are a tight-knit and diverse TK-8th community based in Pasadena.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Alma Fuerte,

Thank you for your response.

This Public Records Act request is not intended to be limited to any particular contractor, company, or individual. While I am not familiar with the specific reference to “Done and Done” mentioned in your response, if that is a contractor, vendor, company, or individual/entity that performed construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work on school property during the requested time period, then records relating to that work would be responsive and should be included. However, this request is not limited to only one specific entity.

To clarify, the request seeks records relating to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on Alma Fuerte school property from January 1, 2023 to present, including:

1. Records identifying each campus construction, renovation, repair, or facilities project, including location and description of work;

2. Records identifying the companies, contractors, vendors, or individuals who performed such work, including associated license numbers, insurance documentation, permits, inspections, approvals, or related compliance documentation;

3. Contracts, agreements, work authorizations, invoices, purchase orders, or procurement records relating to such work;

4. Communications or records reflecting authorization, coordination, approval, or performance of such projects.

The original request was submitted on January 15, 2026. Despite the indication that prior communications were intercepted by a spam filter, the school ultimately did provide a response approximately two months after the original request. However, the records produced consisted primarily of general Amazon purchase records that did not appear responsive to the categories requested above.

Under the California Public Records Act, agencies are required to make a determination within 10 days of receipt of a request and may extend that determination period only as permitted by law. The Act does not permit delay or obstruction of access to public records, nor does it provide an exception allowing a public school to avoid those obligations based on staffing limitations, school size, or the need to search ordinary procurement and facilities records.

The requested records concern recent construction and facilities work and involve basic procurement, contractor, and compliance records that would ordinarily be maintained in the regular course of school operations. Given that the original request was submitted four months ago, and the school has already had substantial time to identify and produce responsive records, an open-ended production timeline extending several additional months is not reasonable.

If certain items require additional time to compile, responsive records should be produced on a rolling basis, beginning with items #1 and #2 above, along with a specific date for the first production and any subsequent rolling productions.

Thank you for your continued attention to the request.

Steve Bennett

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Leon,

I am following up regarding my 05/13/2026 message re-clarifying the scope of this Public Records Act request initially made on 01/15/2026 and requesting dates for rolling production of responsive records.

As of today, 05/21/2026, no further response, production timeline, or rolling production date has been received.

Please provide:

1. A date for the first rolling production of responsive records; and
2. An estimated schedule for any subsequent productions.

Thank you.

Best regards,
Steve Bennett

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Leon and Alma Fuerte,

Thank you for providing the recent production responsive to the Public Records Act request regarding construction, renovation, repair, and facilities-related work performed on school property from January 1, 2023 to present.

Following review of the production, the documents appear to consist primarily of email communications discussing facilities-related work, invoices, quotes, and payments. Some records include formal contractor licensing, insurance, and compliance-related documentation for certain vendors, though similar documentation does not appear to have been included for many of the other facilities projects reflected in the production.

The materials also appear to reflect different channels being utilized for facilities-related work, including formal Pasadena USD/Facilitron work-order communications in some instances, and more informal email-based coordination in others. The informal communications appear to concern numerous campus projects involving walls/windows, fencing/gates, sinks/plumbing, peeling paint/classroom painting, shade structures, security/camera items, flooring, and other facilities work.

Additionally, several emails appear to reference attachments, linked documents, spreadsheets, photographs, project trackers, quotes, and other supporting materials that do not appear to have been included in the production.

To the extent responsive records exist for the above categories, including contractor licensing, insurance, permits, inspections, contracts/work authorizations, procurement approvals, project tracking records, and related compliance documentation, please supplement the production accordingly. If no such records exist for particular identified projects, please indicate that as well.

If any responsive categories are still being gathered or reviewed, please provide an estimated timeline for any supplemental production.

Thank you,
Steve Bennett

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Leon,

I am following up regarding my June 1, 2026 email concerning the recent PRA production request from January 15, 2026

Please let me know whether additional responsive records are being gathered or whether the production is considered complete.

Thank you,
Steve Bennett

communications.subject: Re: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello Steve,

Thank you for your patience, last week was the last week of school which
included 3 graduations. I've asked our backoffice to see if there is an
easy way to download all invoice PDFs and will reply here and add them to
the folder once that is done.

The majority of our school repairs are done by Pasadena Unified as they are
our landlord. They take care of almost everything and there would not be
any contracts/ licenses/ insurance docs that we have for any of the people
that they send out.

I added the google doc and pictured referenced in the emails with LACOE to
the PRA folder. Please let me know if you need any other clarifications.

-- 

Adriani Leon,
Community Schools Director, Hablo Español!
almafuerteps.org

Alma Fuerte is a public school with a focus on entrepreneurship!
We are a tight-knit and diverse TK-8th community based in Pasadena.

title: Financial aid optimization contracts

communications.subject: New Jersey Open Public Records Act Request: Financial aid optimization contracts
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of all active and executed contracts, Master Service Agreements (MSAs), Statements of Work (SOWs), and purchase orders between the University and any third-party vendors (including but not limited to Huron Consulting Group, EAB, or Noel-Levitz/RNL, Encoura, Risepoint (Academic Partnerships) specifically relating to 'financial aid optimization,' 'financial aid leveraging,' or 'financial aid offer letter design and communication strategies.' for the 2024-25, 2025-26, and 2026-27 academic years."

Please also include Vendor Disclosure Statement and any conflict-of-interest filings

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Meredith Kolodner

If you are a first time user and did not submit your request through the public portal but are receiving this e-mail, an account has been created for you with the login and password listed above. At this account you can access, track and receive any documents in response to your request. 
Please log in to the Rutgers University Open Public Records Center and then "My Request Center" to update any contact or password information on your account and to view your request. (https://rutgers.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP/_rs/SupportHome.aspx) 
The University Custodian of Records will contact you if there are questions regarding your request or provide you a response. If you have questions, you may contact the OPRA Administrator at 973.972.8000. 
 
Thank you again for registering with the Rutgers University Open Public Records Center.

Your request was received through the online, public portal Rutgers University Open Public Records Center of this office on 5/31/2026 and  given the reference number R010925-053126. 

Your request will be forwarded to the Office of the Custodian of Records for review and processing, including determining the volume of responsive documents and any potential costs associated with satisfying your request. Please note, the N.J. Open Public Records Act does not require the University to create new documents, to conduct research, or to answer questions for information.

Meredith Kolodner

--- Please respond above this line --- 

Reference: Rutgers Open Public Records Request,  # R010925-053126 
Dear Mrs. Kolodner, 
Please accept this letter as the University Custodian of Records response to your request for records pursuant to the Open Public Records Act (“OPRA”). On May 31, 2026, this office received your request for the following: 
“To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

I am requesting a copy of all active and executed contracts, Master Service Agreements (MSAs), Statements of Work (SOWs), and purchase orders between the University and any third-party vendors (including but not limited to Huron Consulting Group, EAB, or Noel-Levitz/RNL, Encoura, Risepoint (Academic Partnerships) specifically relating to 'financial aid optimization,' 'financial aid leveraging,' or 'financial aid offer letter design and communication strategies.' for the 2024-25, 2025-26, and 2026-27 academic years." 

Please also include Vendor Disclosure Statement and any conflict-of-interest filings 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Meredith Kolodner 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2303550/” 
The University has reviewed your request. The responsive document(s) is available to you through the Rutgers University Open Records Center. Please log in to the Records Center and "My Request Center" to retrieve the appropriate responsive document(s). The document(s) is also attached to this email for your convenience. 
If your request has been denied, you may take your appeal to the New Jersey Government Records Council (NJGRC), which has mediation and dispute resolution procedures, or you may file a lawsuit with the New Jersey Superior Court, as provided by N.J.S.A. 47:1A-1, et seq. Your rights of appeal is provided with this letter at Public Facing Pages of the Rutgers University Open Public Records Request Center . You may also review the brochure at the Rutgers Custodian of Records page or visit the  NJGRC page. 
If you have any questions, or wish to discuss this further, you may contact my office. 
Sincerely, 
Mary Ann Keys 
Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey

title: Library Contract Request: Interfolio (Tarleton State University)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Library Contract Request: Interfolio (Tarleton State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with Elsevier relating to the licensing of Interfolio;
All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; 
All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with Elsevier for faculty information system technology; 
and 
Assessment documentation including, but not limited to, the Higher Education Community Vendor Assessment Toolkit (HECVAT). 

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of Interfolio.

The scope of this request should encompass more than just direct contracts or renewals between the university and Elsevier; please incorporate all applicable agreements and supporting records facilitated through consortium entities like Lyrasis.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Orlando D-FOIA

communications.subject: Tarleton State University Public Information Request :: L002810-060326
communications.communication:
Dear Orlando  D-FOIA: 

We received your public information request for Tarleton State University.  Your request was given the reference number L002810-060326 for tracking purposes.  Please refer to this number when making inquiries about your request. 

You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 

Thank you for your interest in Tarleton State University.

communications.subject: :: L002810-060326
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
06/10/2026 
Orlando D - FOIA (Open Records No. L002810-060326), The responsive documents to your open records request L002810-060326 have been uploaded into the portal. There is no charge for this information. The hyperlink for the portal is: https://www.tarleton.edu/compliance/open-records/ From the link above, click on “the Open Records Request Portal” and then select “My Request Center” to access this open records request using the assigned reference number above . This documentation may also be made available upon request by inspection or duplication or by receipt through United States Mail, as provided by section 552.221(b) of the Texas Government Code (Gov’t Code).  Please let us know if you have any issues accessing the requested documentation in the portal or if you would like to review the documentation in another format as per section 552.221(b) of the Gov’t Code by contacting openrecords@tarleton.edu (mailto:openrecords@tarleton.edu) . Regarding the Master Service Agreement with vendor “Interfolio”, The Texas A&M University System would be the provider of any applicable responsive documents to your request.  Please submit your open records request to the Texas A&M System Office by using this link: https://www.tamus.edu/legal/open-records/open-records-request/  This constitutes the closure of Open Records Request L002810-060326. Sincerely, 

Kent Styron Public Information Officer 
Tarleton State University – Open Records

title: City of Andrews, TX PIR Request

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: City of Andrews, TX PIR Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period from October 17, 2025 to April 22, 2026 please provide all logs and available related details of public records requests received by this office. Responsive materials include those that describe the following details for each public records request received by this office:
The name of the requester and the affiliated organization
The date on which the request was received by this office
A description of the requested records
The tracking number assigned by this office to the request
The date on which the request was closed by this office
The final disposition of the request, including, if possible, whether the request was fully fulfilled, partially fulfilled, rejected in its entirety, resulted in no responsive documents, or remains open
Details on exemptions applied to responsive and withheld records
The number of released pages

This request is being made as part of an academic and journalistic project and is not being made for any commercial reason.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Justin Huertas

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Sacramento Police Department public records request #26-608
communications.communication:
Sacramento Police Department ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-608: Date: March 12, 2026 Public Records Reference #26-608 Maxine Doogan: This letter is in response to your California Public Records Act request dated March 4, 2026, wherein you requested the following: "1) All citation and arrest statistics for California Penal Code Section 647(b) and 653.25 for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026. Please provide all segregable related data, including the case number, the date and time on which the arrest or citation occurred, the officers involved, gender of those sited, DOB, race, name and the location of the occurrence. If your agency worked in collaboration with another law enforcement agency on investigations involving violations of California Penal Code Section 647(b) and 653.25, please provide the case numbers for these investigations and all relevant associated material, even if this agency was not the lead investigatory agency or the agency that executed the arrest or citation. 2) All police and incident reports and field interview cards associated with all citations and arrests conducted in reference to violations of 647(b) and 653.25. Please also provide all incident reports (or equivalent summaries of police interactions) related to violations of 647(b) and 653.25 for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026. 3) All citation and arrest statistics for California Penal Code Section 236.1 for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026. Please provide all segregable related data, including the case number, the date and time on which the arrest or citation occurred, the officers involved, gender of those sited, DOB, race, name and the location of the occurrence. If your agency worked in collaboration with another law enforcement agency on investigations involving violations of California Penal Code Section 653.25, please provide the case numbers for these investigations and all relevant associated material, even if this agency was not the lead investigatory agency or the agency that executed the arrest or citation. 4) All police and incident reports and field interview cards associated with all citations and arrests conducted in reference to violations of 236.1. Please also provide all incident reports (or equivalent summaries of police interactions) related to violations of 236.1 for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026 5) Provide all MOUs, contracts, agreements, reports including financial documents for all prostitution diversion programs for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026. 6)Provide all MOUs, contracts, agreements, reports including financial documents for all partnerships with human trafficking task forces including all training materials for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026." The Sacramento Police Department has begun researching and identifying records responsive to your request. Consequently, additional time is necessary to properly respond to your request due to the volume of materials required to be searched, collected, and examined for responsiveness (permitted under Government Code 7922.535 (a)). We will notify you within fourteen (14) days as to whether the City is in possession of non-exempt records responsive to your request. ​Thank you,​ Anna Johnson Sacramento Police Department Transparency and Compliance 5770 Freeport Blvd., Suite 100 Sacramento, CA 95822 ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Sacramento Police Department.

Technical support: See our help page

Please provide all segregable related data, including the case number, the date and time on which the arrest or citation occurred, the officers involved, gender of those sited, DOB, race, name and the location of the occurrence. Please also provide all incident reports (or equivalent summaries of police interactions) related to violations of 647(b) and 653.25 for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026. 3) All citation and arrest statistics for California Penal Code Section 236.1 for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026. Please provide all segregable related data, including the case number, the date and time on which the arrest or citation occurred, the officers involved, gender of those sited, DOB, race, name and the location of the occurrence.
