title: FOIA Request: Dearborn

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Dearborn
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of invoice/purchase order #3008736230, dated 5/20/2025 for $20,000 from the City of Dearborn.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: Re: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Dearborn
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Regarding your Freedom of Information Act request below, we anticipate that
we will reply to your request electronically. However, pursuant to Section
3 (1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, a request from a person
"must include the requesting person's complete name, address, and contact
information," and an address "must be written in compliance with United
States Postal Service addressing standards." Contact information "must
include a valid telephone number or electronic mail address."

Please provide the missing information as required by the statute.

Thank you,

Matthew Riggs
FOIA Compliance Specialist Associate

Suite 3300 Ruthven | 1109 Geddes Ave. | Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1079

Please send records requests to: foia-email@umich.edu



[image: A blue text on a black backgroundAI-generated content may be
incorrect.]

In order to submit a valid Freedom of Information Act request, a requester
“must include the requesting person’s complete name, address, and contact
information." An address "must be written in compliance with United States
Postal Service addressing standards." Contact information "must include a
valid telephone number or electronic mail address." Since we have already
received your mailing address and email address, please provide your
complete name.

We will process your request once we receive the information required by
the statute.

Thank you,

Matthew Riggs

communications.communication:
Dear Ed Vogel,

I am writing in regard to your Freedom of Information Act request dated May
21, 2026, which was received on May 22, 2026.

Due to the large number of requests currently being processed by this
office, it will not be possible to respond to this request within the
five-day period accorded by the Michigan Freedom of Information Act.
However, under Section 5 (2) (d) of the Act, the University is permitted to
extend the deadline for not more than 10 business days.

communications.subject: Public Information Request :: W030457-052626
communications.communication:
Dear e e: 
Welcome to the City of North Richland Hills Public Information Request Center and thank you for your interest in public records of the City of North Richland Hills. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Chapter 552 of the Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act.  Your request was received in this office on 5/26/2026 9:37:30 PM and given the reference number W030457-052626 for tracking purposes. Records Requested:  To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

A copy of all documents, including but not limited to, applications, agreements, and reporting documents related to the award for the "Texas Anti-Gang Program, FY2026" submitted to the Governor's Public Safety Office. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2299487/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant North Richland Hills TX Records Office department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the North Richland Hills Public Information Request Center. 
 
To track your request and respond, visit the North Richland Hills Public Information Request Center, click on " My Records Center." 
 
**If you requested plans for residential or commercial buildings, please note that some plans may be stamped proprietary and copyright by the architect.  The City of North Richland Hills will comply with the Architectural Works and Copyright Protection Act of 1990.  This will also apply to homeowners requesting copies of proprietary or copyright plans. 
For more information on the Texas Public Information Act, please visit the Texas Attorney General website. 
 
Sincerely, 
City of North Richland Hills 
(817) 427-6000

--- Please respond above this line --- 
Attached, please find your Open Record Request. 
 
  *  We are withholding the following under Mandatory Exceptions: 
 
  *  Information pertaining to an ongoing investigation detailed in the Q1 report pursuant to section 552.108(a)(1) of the Government Code. 
  *  Access information on the application pursuant to section 552.136 of the Government Code. 
 We are withholding the following under Discretionary Exceptions: 
 
  *  Information regarding software and devices used by undercover officers as well as police tactics detailed in the responsive documents pursuant to section 552.108(b)(1) of the Government Code. How to appeal the withholding of information: If you wish to appeal the withholding of information discussed on the previous page, you must send the following to the attorney general: 1) a signed, written statement indicating your wish to appeal the withholding of information; 2) the name of the governmental body that withheld information from you; 3) the date you made your original request for information; and 4) a copy of your original request for information, or if you are unable to provide a copy, a description of your original request for information. You may also submit written comments stating why you think the information should be released to you, but you are not required to do so. Send your appeal by mail to the attorney general at: Open Records Division P.O. Box 12548 Austin, Texas 78711-2548 Within forty-five business days after receiving all of the above-listed items necessary to file your appeal, the attorney general will issue a written ruling on the matter. You will receive a copy of this ruling in the mail. Sincerely, Jennifer Brantley 
Police Records

title: Principal Newsletter, Memos

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Principal Newsletter, Memos
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

CPS leadership sends a principal's weekly newsletter and memos to principals intermittently.

communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
06/09/2026 
Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019675-052526 The CPS FOIA Office has reviewed your request and has determined the following: 
1. Records sufficient to show principal weekly newsletters issued in SY 2025-2026. 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. 
Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5). 2. Records sufficient to show any memos that were issued to CPS principals in SY 2025-2026.  
RESPONSE: The CPS FOIA Office has reviewed this portion of your request and determined that it fails to cite a public record for review, and instead essentially seeks the answers to questions posed, outside the scope of the FOIA. In order to answer your questions, the District would be required to inquire with every department to inquire whether a memo was issued, and then combine that information into a new record, not maintained in the normal course of business and outside the scope of the FOIA. Thus, your request is asking the District to create a new record to answer your questions. Additionally, be advised that the FOIA does not require the District to conduct calculations, create an analysis, nor conduct research to answer your questions. As determined in Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32 (4th Dist. 1989), the FOIA applies to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request. Further, it has been determined that requesting a tally that a public body has not already prepared does not cite a public record for review as required under the FOIA and performing a review of records to prepare a tally constitutes preparing a new record, outside the scope of the FOIA. [Chi. Tribune Co. v. Dep't of Fin. & Prof'l Regulation, 2014 IL App (4th) 130427, 8 N.E.3d 11, 2014 Ill. App. LEXIS 120, 380 Ill. Dec. 80, 2014 WL 895023]. 
You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2298763/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. Student/parent information has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). title: Community Crime Map - LexisNexis Data Sharing Agreements

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Community Crime Map - LexisNexis Data Sharing Agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) All contracts, addendums, schedule agreements, and amendments between your agency and LexisNexis (including RELX, LexisNexis Risk Solutions, and Accurint)
2) Technical integration documents between your agency's RMS/CAD systems and LexisNexis. Common integration documents include API specifications, Interface Control Document (ICD), data translation guides, field mapping tables, schemas, or data interface documents that identify what data your agency is sharing with LexisNexis. 

Context: Your agency uses LexisNexis's Community Crime Map, which means some of your city's police data is shared with LexisNexis. We seek to understand exactly what data your agency shares with LexisNexis, what the scope of data sharing is, and if you contribute data to LexisNexis's Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (St. Tammany Parish Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (St. Tammany Parish Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Enforcement Public Records :: R026788-050626
communications.communication:
Thank you for visiting the St. Tammany Parish Sheriff's Office Public Records Center.  Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Louisiana Public Records Law.  Your request was received in this office on 5/6/2026 and assigned the reference number R026788-050626 for tracking purposes. Type of Records:  Other (Please specify in the description box below) Report / Case No.: Records Requested:  Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved.  If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request.  If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers.  Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request.  I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.  I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires. Inmate Name: Defendant's Name: 
Your request will be routed to all relevant department(s) for processing.  Please note that the St. Tammany Parish Sheriff’s Office will avail itself of any provision of law which either exempts or prohibits production of documents pursuant to state law and the Louisiana Constitution.  Once the research has been concluded, our agency will notify you via email regarding the availability of the requested records and the status of your request. 
Monitoring the progress of your request is available by using the link below.  Again, thank you for using the St. Tammany Parish Sheriff’s Office Public Records Center. 
St. Tammany Parish Sheriff's Office 
PLEASE NOTE:  Louisiana Public Records Law does not require a governmental body to create a new document in response to a request for public records. To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the St. Tammany Parish Sheriff's Office Public Records Center

communications.subject: [STPSO Records Center] Enforcement Public Records :: R026788-050626
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: Public Records Request of May 06, 2026, Reference # R026788-050626 Mr. Sinyangwe, Your correspondence dated April 29, 2026 was received by the St. Tammany Parish Sheriff's Office (STPSO) Legal Department this morning.  In your correspondence, you indicate that your request was originally submitted on January 28, 2026.  However, the original request was not properly submitted through our online public records center and therefore, was never received.  For future reference, a request can only be submitted by logging into an established customer account via the "Submit a Request" feature.  Sending a random email (not including replying to a request communication email message such as this one) to the domain stpso@govqa.us will receive an automated message stating "You have responded to an unresponsive email.  If you wish to submit a public records request, update an existing request, or check the status of an already submitted request, please click here to login to the St. Tammany Parish Sheriff's Office Public Records Center.  Thank you."  Our records indicate that you received this automated response on 1/28/26.  Nevertheless, your mail request has been received this date and will be placed in line for processing. Be advised that the records sought in your request will require coordination with multiple departments within the agency.  Inter-departmental collaboration as well as the extensive temporal limit and criteria of the records sought may require additional processing time and could possibly result in higher production fees.  However, once the records have been identified and inventoried, an agency invoice will be sent to you via portal message as advance payment is required.  Should you have any questions in the meantime, please advise via an email reply to this message or by using the messaging feature within the Public Records Center after logging into your account. Sincerely, Jeannine Buckner Legal Department

title: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails between Travis Johnson, Chief of Staff to Attorney General Chris Carr, and Frank Russo (also known as Frank W. Russo Jr.), Partner/Vice President of Modern Fortis, from January 1, 2025 through the date of this request.

"frank@modernfortis.com" <frank@modernfortis.com>

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Georgia Open Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on May 14, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication: This acknowledges receipt of your request under the Open Records Act and provides our required initial response.  Our office requires additional time to search and review our records to response to this request.  We anticipate that our initial review will take at least ten business days.  Responsive records, if any, may contain materials that are exempt from disclosure under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72 or other applicable law including, but not limited to, materials that are exempt as work product or attorney-client privileged.    We anticipate that our charge to comply with your request will exceed $100.00.   Please advise whether you will be responsible for this cost.  Please also advise as to the status of your payment of prior amounts incurred.   We anticipate providing our best time and cost estimate for completing your request in the next 10 business days.  We reserve the right to provide a revision as our review progresses.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
Thank you for your acknowledgment. Please note that I have mailed checks for any previously required payments.

I write to respectfully push back on the anticipated cost estimate exceeding $100. This request is narrowly scoped to emails between two specific individuals — Travis Johnson and Frank Russo at frank@modernfortis.com — over a defined 16-month period. A simple email search by sender and recipient should require minimal staff time and no extraordinary resources. I would ask that you provide a specific, itemized cost estimate as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71 before proceeding.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
We have completed our search and review.  The cost of processing your request is $44.15, which includes 0.67 hours of administrative time at a rate of $20 per hour and 0.50 hours of attorney management time at a rate of $61.50 per hour.

We redacted records containing cell phone numbers under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(20).

If you wish to proceed, please confirm in writing that you will be responsible for payment of the costs of response.


Cordially,

Open Records
Office of the Attorney General
Georgia Department of Law
40 Capitol Square SW
Atlanta, Georgia 30334

If you wish to proceed, please confirm in writing that you will be responsible for payment of the costs of response.  Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Travis Johnson Frank Russo Modern Fortis Communication
communications.communication:
Thank you for the updated estimate. 

However, the entire billable time — 0.25 hours (15 minutes) of administrative time — falls within the mandatory free period under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(c), which requires the first 15 minutes of search, retrieval, and redaction time to be provided at no charge. There is no remaining billable time. Please produce the five responsive records at no cost.

Jason Hunyar

title: Law enforcement informal and formal settlement data and reports (Sparks Police Department)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Law enforcement informal and formal settlement data and reports (Sparks Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Materials related to and sufficient to show all instances of alleged or actual inappropriate or illegal behavior or actions resulting in compensation or expenditures involving this agency. 

Responsive materials include records of all settlements (formal and informal) and payments to which this agency was or is a party for the period from January 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed. Responsive materials include those for matters and legal claims resolved prior to the initiation of any litigation. 

In particular, materials responsive to this request include all reports, lists, databases, spreadsheets, and other documentation detailing incidents of legal claims or other allegations requiring compensation for claimants, attorneys, or any other party. 

If spreadsheets or similar documents are available in response to this request, please provide all segregable data, including but not limited to:

The nature of the claim or legal matter
The claimant and other parties involved
The relevant case and tracking numbers for court and internal systems
The date the lawsuit was served or litigation commenced
The final disposition or current status of the case, including whether a case is still pending
The amount spent to date as a result of the legal engagement
The amount paid to any parties related to the legal claim, including any city payout and any non-monetary compensation

If clarification of this request is required, please respond to the provided email address or leave a message at 617-299-1832.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Annemarie Grant

 The record(s) you have been provided do not contain any documentation from other agencies or any confidential information.  Sparks Police Department  is required to redact or remove all personal identifying information (NRS 603A.040), proprietary information (NRS 333.335), information from the National Crime Information Center (28 CFR § 50.12(b); USC 28 § 534(f)(1); CJIS Security Policy Section 4.2.3), information about ongoing criminal investigations (NRS 199.520; Donrey of Nevada v. Bradshaw, 106 Nev. 630, 798 P.2d 144 (1990) ), information about victims of crimes (Nevada Constitution, Article 1, Section 23; NRS 178.5691); and information relating to individuals’ private interests such that disclosure to the public would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of privacy (Clark Cnty. Sch. Dist. v. Las Vegas Review-Journal, 134 Nev. 700, 429 P.3d 313 (2018)). 
 
 If your request contained any of the above information, it has been redacted or removed as required by law.

title: County and Municipal Budgets

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: County and Municipal Budgets
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All county and municipal budgets from year 2012-2013 to 2025-2026 for each county and municipal government as listed on the State Auditor’s website at https://www.wvsao.gov/LocalGovernment/Default. I’m requesting this data because there is not a bulk download button on the website, and the process of gathering and sending the data to me should be more simple for you than for me to individually download or scrape your website. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. On the State Auditor’s website, these files are provided as PDFs. However, I suspect that the raw data is stored in a tabular data format like spreadsheets or a relational database. If this the case, please note that I am requesting the tabular or native digital format and not the PDF reports. If there you are not in possession of any responsive records other than the PDFs, then I am requesting the PDFs themselves. 

If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Emails sent to or from* any of the officials (and/or their staffers) listed below and any email address ending with mfa.gov.il or gov.il:

•  Daniel Perez
•  Tyler Sirois 
•  Chuck Brannan
•  Hillary Cassel

•  Mike Caruso

•  Chip LaMarca
•  Michelle Salzman
•  Debra Tendrich
•  Mitch Rosenwald
•  Kelly Skidmore
•  Allison Tant
•  Katherine Waldron
•  Jeff Holcomb
•  Michael Gottlieb
•  Jennifer Canady
•  Vicki Lopez
•  Robbie Brackett
•  Tiffany Esposito

* By "sent to," I also mean emails CC'd or BCC'd to the people named.

You may limit the search to the dates between 1 January 2026 and the date this request is processed.

If any emails contain attachments, please include those attachments as well. If some portions of the requested records are properly exempt from disclosure, please disclose any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of the requested records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: PRR 198 Matthew Petti (Speaker Perez, et al. - mfa.gov.il)
communications.communication:
The Office of Open Government received the request below.  Records maintained by the House on the House server will be searched for public records that meet the criteria of the request.  Any such records will be provided to you in accordance with Article 1, Section 24(c) of the Florida Constitution; section 11.0431, Florida Statutes; and House Rules 14.1 and 14.2.

For your information, requests for public records that are maintained by a member, including text messages, social media posts, paper records, and emails to or from personal email accounts, must be submitted directly to the member.

Kind regards,

Office of Open Government
Florida House of Representatives

Please Note: The Florida Constitution requires disclosure of public records unless a Florida Statute exempts the records from the disclosure requirement.  Most emails to and from House members and staff that were sent or received in connection with the transaction of legislative business are public records that will be made available to the public and media upon request.  Therefore, the contents of your email and your email address are subject to public disclosure unless a specific statute exempts them from the Constitution’s disclosure requirements.

title: Flock Safety audits, network sharing, and Grayson area cameras

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Safety audits, network sharing, and Grayson area cameras
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to this agency’s use of, access to, or data-sharing through Flock Safety, Flock Group, automated license plate readers, ALPR systems, vehicle recognition cameras, and related camera-sharing networks.

For the period January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed, please provide:

1. Organization Audit in Flock. The report should include searches or lookups performed by this agency’s users, including all available fields such as date, time, user, reason/purpose, search type, query type, case number or justification field, and whether the search was manual, alert based, hotlist based, or investigative.

2. Network Audit in Flock. The report should include searches, lookups, hits, alerts, or access to this agency’s Flock network by any other agency, organization, jurisdiction, user group, or external partner.

3. Current Flock network sharing settings, including:
   a. Flock networks shared with this agency;
   b. Flock networks this agency shares with others;
   c. devices, camera groups, or locations included in each share;
   d. permissions granted, including search, alert, hotlist, live-view, investigative search, export, administrative, or similar permissions;
   e. any national, statewide, federal, or out-of-state sharing settings.

4. A copy of Flock Safety’s Settings & Configuration export for this agency, if available from the Flock Admin menu or any similar administrative/export function.

5. Records sufficient to show every Flock Safety, ALPR, or vehicle recognition camera owned, operated, accessed, funded, approved, or shared with this agency in or affecting Grayson, Georgia, the 30017 ZIP code, Bay Creek Precinct territory, or the surrounding unincorporated Gwinnett area. If exact coordinates are withheld, please provide the least-redacted existing record showing camera identifier, road/intersection, city or ZIP code, owner/operator, install date, and whether the camera is government-owned, HOA-owned, business-owned, school-owned, or privately shared.

6. Current policies, standard operating procedures, general orders, training materials, user guides, retention policies, hotlist policies, audit policies, misuse policies, and disciplinary policies governing Flock Safety, ALPR systems, vehicle recognition cameras, or camera-sharing systems.

7. Current contracts, purchase orders, invoices, quotes, statements of work, renewal documents, grants, memoranda of understanding, data-sharing agreements, intergovernmental agreements, letters of intent, and other agreement documents with Flock Safety, Flock Group, or any third party relating to Flock/ALPR services.

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine-readable format where available, including CSV, XLSX, PDF, native export, database export, or other spreadsheet format. Please do not redirect me to a public transparency portal in place of producing responsive records.

If any portion of a record is withheld, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and cite the specific Georgia Code section, subsection, and paragraph relied upon for each withholding or redaction.

Please notify me before incurring costs over $25. If any part of this request is considered too broad, please process items 1, 2, 3, and 4 first and contact me with a proposed narrowed scope for the remaining items.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Don Page

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2307514/

Gwinnett County

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request #R278783-060326
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Landreau,

Thank you for your response.

I respectfully request reconsideration or clarification of the blanket denial. I understand that certain operational details, specific sensitive camera locations, tactical security plans, vulnerability assessments, or security device details may be subject to redaction or withholding under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(25)(A). However, the request also seeks administrative, financial, policy, contractual, and non sensitive records that do not appear to fall entirely within that exemption.

Please process and produce all reasonably segregable, non exempt portions of responsive records, including but not limited to:

Current contracts, purchase orders, invoices, quotes, statements of work, renewal documents, grants, memoranda of understanding, data sharing agreements, intergovernmental agreements, letters of intent, and other agreement documents with Flock Safety, Flock Group, or any third party relating to Flock/ALPR services;
Current policies, standard operating procedures, general orders, retention policies, hotlist policies, audit policies, misuse policies, disciplinary policies, and training materials governing Flock Safety, ALPR systems, vehicle recognition cameras, or camera sharing systems, with any specific tactical/security sensitive portions redacted as needed;
Non sensitive records sufficient to show whether Gwinnett County Police uses, accesses, funds, contracts for, or shares data through Flock Safety or ALPR systems;
Non sensitive records sufficient to show the existence of any Flock/ALPR network sharing agreements or partner agencies, with any specific tactical details redacted as needed.

If the Department maintains that every responsive record is fully exempt, please provide a more specific explanation of how each category of requested records falls within O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(25)(A), including which subparagraph applies to contracts, invoices, purchase orders, general policies, and non sensitive administrative records.

To narrow the request and reduce any security concerns, I am willing to defer camera-location details, audit logs, and operational search logs for now. Please first process the administrative/financial/policy records listed above.

Please notify me before incurring costs over $25.

Sincerely,

Don Page

For the period January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed, please provide: 

1. Organization Audit in Flock. The report should include searches or lookups performed by this agency’s users, including all available fields such as date, time, user, reason/purpose, search type, query type, case number or justification field, and whether the search was manual, alert based, hotlist based, or investigative. 

2. Network Audit in Flock. The report should include searches, lookups, hits, alerts, or access to this agency’s Flock network by any other agency, organization, jurisdiction, user group, or external partner. 

3. Current Flock network sharing settings, including: 
a. Flock networks shared with this agency; 
b. Flock networks this agency shares with others; 
c. devices, camera groups, or locations included in each share; 
d. permissions granted, including search, alert, hotlist, live-view, investigative search, export, administrative, or similar permissions; 
e. any national, statewide, federal, or out-of-state sharing settings. 

4. A copy of Flock Safety’s Settings & Configuration export for this agency, if available from the Flock Admin menu or any similar administrative/export function. 

5. Records sufficient to show every Flock Safety, ALPR, or vehicle recognition camera owned, operated, accessed, funded, approved, or shared with this agency in or affecting Grayson, Georgia, the 30017 ZIP code, Bay Creek Precinct territory, or the surrounding unincorporated Gwinnett area. If exact coordinates are withheld, please provide the least-redacted existing record showing camera identifier, road/intersection, city or ZIP code, owner/operator, install date, and whether the camera is government-owned, HOA-owned, business-owned, school-owned, or privately shared. 

6. Current policies, standard operating procedures, general orders, training materials, user guides, retention policies, hotlist policies, audit policies, misuse policies, and disciplinary policies governing Flock Safety, ALPR systems, vehicle recognition cameras, or camera-sharing systems. 

7. Current contracts, purchase orders, invoices, quotes, statements of work, renewal documents, grants, memoranda of understanding, data-sharing agreements, intergovernmental agreements, letters of intent, and other agreement documents with Flock Safety, Flock Group, or any third party relating to Flock/ALPR services. 

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine-readable format where available, including CSV, XLSX, PDF, native export, database export, or other spreadsheet format. Please do not redirect me to a public transparency portal in place of producing responsive records. 

If any portion of a record is withheld, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and cite the specific Georgia Code section, subsection, and paragraph relied upon for each withholding or redaction. 

Please notify me before incurring costs over $25. If any part of this request is considered too broad, please process items 1, 2, 3, and 4 first and contact me with a proposed narrowed scope for the remaining items. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Don Page 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2307514/" 
Gwinnett County has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request. Portions of the records requested are exempt from disclosure under the Georgia Open Records Act and have been excluded for the following reason: FLOCK: O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72(a)(25)(A) Records the disclosure of which would compromise security against sabotage or criminal or terrorist acts and the nondisclosure of which is necessary for the protection of life, safety, or public property which shall be limited to the following: (iii) Any document relating to the existence, nature, location, or function of security devices designed to protect against terrorist or other attacks that depend for their effectiveness in whole or in part upon a lack of general public knowledge; 
I have added the County contracts regarding FLOCK. 
Please log in to the Open Records Center at the following link to retrieve the appropriate responsive documents. Public Records Request - R278783-060326 Gwinnett County Public Records Center 
 
  * Click on My Request Center 
  * Click on View My Requests - Choose the request you want to view. 
  * Click on View Files 
 
  * Your request will open, and any files/attachments associated with the request. Click on the title of the document you wish to download from the “Files” box. 
  *  If you are using Chrome as your browser, the documents will download at the bottom of the screen. You will need to click on the document(s) at the bottom of the screen once it downloads to open/view the file. If the files do not appear to be downloading, please check your firewall and pop-up blocker settings. Note: 
If you have any questions or need additional information, please feel free to contact my office at 770.513.5114. 
Sincerely, 
Susan Landreau 
Customer Service Asc III 
Police

title: Request to Department of Defense for 2025 budget of the Office of the Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Department of Defense for 2025 budget of the Office of the Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The 2025 annual budget for the Office of the Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: William M. Beecher (May 27, 1933 – February 9, 2024)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: William M. Beecher (May 27, 1933 – February 9, 2024)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into investigative journalist William M. Beecher (May 27, 1933 – February 9, 2024). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2024/02/18/business/media/william-beecher-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an investigative journalist who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, Reason Magazine, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-000
communications.communication:
To whom this may concern,

My name is Matthew Petti and I am writing in response to my FOIA request number 1620220-000. Thank you so much for your prompt response.

In your letter dated 6 March 2024, you write that "additional records potentially responsive to your subject may exist. If this release of previously processed material does not satisfy your request, you may request an additional search for records."

I would like to formally advise that I would like an additional search for records. Thank you again for your prompt attention to this matter. If possible, please let me know if you've received this message.

Best regards,
Matthew

communications.subject: FOIA Request 1620220-001/William Beecher
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

We're contacting you in reference to the FOIPA request number included in the subject line of this email.  The FBI located approximately 2,083 pages potentially responsive to your request. 

Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system:
Extra-small track requests (0-50 pages) current average time is approximately 8 months to complete;
Small track requests (51-250 pages) current average time is approximately 36 months to complete;
Medium track requests (251-500 pages) current average time is approximately 44 months to complete;
Large track requests (501-4,999 pages) current average time is approximately 67 months to complete; and
Extra-large track requests (5,000+ pages) current average time is approximately 79 months to complete.

The current average time to complete your large track request is at least 67 months. Reducing the scope of your request may accelerate the processing, allow for a timelier receipt of the information you seek, and reduce the duplication costs, if applicable.  Would you be willing to consider reducing the scope of your request to place it in a smaller, potentially faster processing track?

Please let us know if this is something you would be interested in further discussing. If you chose to narrow the scope of your request, you will be able to request remaining material via a new FOIPA request submission at the completion of this request, subject to applicable fees.

Respectfully,

Negotiation Team
Federal Bureau of Investigation
Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov<mailto:Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov>

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-001
communications.communication:
Dear Negotiation Team,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Matthew Petti and I am writing with regard to my FOIA request number 1620220-001/William Beecher. Do you have any suggestions on how to reduce the scope of the request? Otherwise, I am happy to leave it in the large track.

Sincerely,
Matthew

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1620220-001
communications.communication:
Good morning,
Thank you for your email concerning the scope reduction of this request.  Unfortunately, we are limited on what we can discuss since the potentially responsive pages haven’t been reviewed by a FOIA analyst.
After further review, the potentially responsive pages are within the date range of 1953-1987.
We recommend the following options:

We could narrow the scope of this request by limiting to a specific date range within 1953-1987.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 50 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the simple track with an average time to complete of approximately 8 months.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 250 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the small track with an average time to complete of approximately 36 months.

We could reduce the scope of this request by limiting to approximately 500 potentially responsive pages.  This request would move to the medium track with an average time to complete of approximately 44 months.

Please let us know how you would like to move forward with reducing the scope of your request.
Respectfully,

Negotiation Team
Federal Bureau of Investigation
Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov<mailto:Fbi.foipa.negotiation@fbi.gov>

title: DDA-75-5800 / GAO Audit Task Force final report

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: DDA-75-5800 / GAO Audit Task Force final report
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of DDA-75-5800, the 5 December 1975 final report of the GAO Audit Task Force.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: HPSCI Report on the Implementation of FISA

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: HPSCI Report on the Implementation of FISA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the HPSCI Report on the Implementation of FISA described in CIA-RDP86B00338R000200330012-3.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, AND Magazine, Unicorn Riot, and more. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and and the journalist non-profit, MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: William A Read

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: William A Read
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any records pertaining to William August Read (born August 21, 1895) and his employment by or work for the Central Intelligence Agency or associated projects. He is my great grandfather and there are family stories about his work for the CIA, but I would be very curious around any records of it.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Benjamin Read

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA US Navy records 5/12
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: Nickolas Petersen
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: Dec 21, 1944
Dates of Service: Approx 1963 to 1972

Request following documents:
1) copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
2) History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
3) Navy commendation medal award citations
4) Presidential Unit citation award citation

Historical Documentation
Redact per 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6)
I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.

Please mail hardcopies to:
MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115
or please email pdf copies to requests@muckrock.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley
A copy of documents responsive to the request.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request the listing of OPA allegations in IAPro with a received date on or after January 1, 2025 with columns:
 Occurred date    OPA #    CAD/Incident No    ID number    First name    Last name    Date hired    Incident type    Directive    Finding    Finding date    Action taken    Employee Type 
I request the listing be provided in Excel format so I can easily sort/filter it. Redactions should be done in Excel.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

This email is in further response to your public disclosure records request P255844-020826. The Seattle Police Department has records ready to be released pending payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request. 
 SPD strongly encourages online payments. 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: P255844-020826. 
  * Select the 'View Invoices' button. 
 
  * Opens a pop-up window showing your invoice: INV26-P255844-1. 
  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. After we receive your payment through the Police Records Request Center Secure Payments, the Public Disclosure Unit will release your records within one to two business days and send you a notice with instructions on how to download the records. 
 Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your E-mail contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center E-mails to your Inbox. (mailto:seattle@mycusthelp.net)

RE: Public Disclosure Request#: P255844-020826 
 
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Your records have been released and are available on the Public Records Request Center to download. 
To download your records, please: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Select this request: P255844-020826. 
  * Select the 'View Files' button. 
  * You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. 
If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation. OPA will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so within 10 business days. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. 
Please include your name and address, a copy of any redacted files, and a copy of this letter together with a brief statement identifying the basis of the appeal. 
Please mail or deliver your appeal to:

title: Unredacted contract 3640 between City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Unredacted contract 3640 between City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

An unredacted, complete copy of City of Seattle Supplier Contract #000000000003640 (and all associated change orders) between the City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise, Inc., originally dated on or about November 5, 2024.

To clarify, this request specifically seeks the full, unredacted content of the following sections which were redacted in previous disclosures:
 - Attachment C – Pricing
 - Attachment D – Evidence.com Terms of Use
 - Section 1.26 of Attachment A, Statement of Work (SOW)
 - All Service Level Agreements (SLA) or supplemental terms incorporated by reference into [that] agreement."

Please note:
 - Pricing is Public Post-Award: Under Washington law, pricing for "goods and services" purchased with public funds is not a trade secret under RCW 42.56.270 once a contract has been awarded and executed. The public has a paramount interest in knowing the financial terms of surveillance technology procurement.
 - Terms of Use are Not Exempt: Terms of Use (Attachment D) are not proprietary business methods; they are the legal rules governing the Agency’s use of public data. Under City of Sedro-Woolley v. Rodriguez (2025), data "paid for by" and "generated for the benefit of" the City is a public record regardless of storage location or vendor-imposed terms.
 - Prohibition on Contractual Confidentiality: An agency cannot "contract away" its statutory obligations under the PRA. Any clause in the Axon contract promising confidentiality for non-exempt information is void as a matter of public policy.
 - Narrow Construction of Exemptions: Per RCW 42.56.030, the PRA must be liberally construed in favor of disclosure and exemptions must be interpreted narrowly. The City bears the burden of proving that every redacted sentence—including "Terms of Use"—meets a specific statutory exemption.
 - Constructive Possession: Under the Nissen and O'Neill frameworks, the City maintains "constructive possession" of all terms and data within the Axon/Evidence.com ecosystem. Redacting the terms that define this possession (the SOW and Terms of Use) is a violation of the "fullest assistance" mandate in RCW 42.56.100.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

title: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Yakima Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: ALPR WA 2026: H: All ALPR Scan Records (Yakima Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

## PRESERVATION DEMAND ##

Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt.

Please immediately:

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval.

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution.

## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation.  Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ##

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production.

## FEE REQUEST ##

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

## RECORDS REQUESTED ##

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals.

2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced.

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request:

	Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles
	(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered
	but vehicles are believed to remain in service):
	c. 50816D  — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010)
	d. 50887D  — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031)
	e. 54180D  — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031)
	f. 43500D  — Bomb squad trailer (2005)
	g. 26073D  — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997)
	h. 41461D  — Communications van (2005)
	i. 44372D  — Command vehicle (2005)
	j. 54148D  — Riot fencing trailer (2011)
	k. 54362D  — CSI van with generator (2012)
